response: {"purpose":"Figures 4c–d demonstrate the utility of eCLIP for detecting RNA-binding protein (RBP) interactions with the highly abundant noncoding RNA MALAT1, a locus that is notoriously difficult to assess with standard CLIP approaches due to its high expression. Panel c presents the fold-enrichment of the most enriched peak overlapping MALAT1 across all 204 K562 and HepG2 eCLIP datasets, highlighting RBPs that show specific and reproducible enrichment at this locus. Panel d provides read density tracks along MALAT1 for representative replicates of selected RBPs, illustrating the spatial distribution and specificity of binding sites. Together, these panels establish that the SMInput normalization strategy of eCLIP effectively distinguishes genuine RBP binding from background signal even on highly expressed noncoding RNAs, enabling reliable RNA-centric discovery of functional interactions.","title":"RBP binding enrichment and read density along lincRNA MALAT1"}
